sourceMovie.title: Extortion
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Extortion (2017) stuck because it commits to its action premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Phil Volken's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Mesrine: Killer Instinct
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Extortion clicked for you, Mesrine: Killer Instinct (2008) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and crime, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Extortion?
faq.answer: Try Mesrine: Killer Instinct, Traitor, Suicide Kings—each shares DNA with Extortion while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Extortion?
faq.answer: Extortion (2017) is best described as Action, Adventure, Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Extortion worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
